clusters.leadHeadline: NFL Faces FCC Scrutiny Over Media Rights Strategy
clusters.summary: Federal Communications Commission questions NFL's media distribution approach as league defends its strategy to Washington. Regulatory pressure could reshape how leagues negotiate broadcasting rights.

clusters.leadHeadline: All 44 Caitlin Clark Fever Games Go National TV in WNBA Deal
clusters.summary: Unprecedented national TV coverage for single player marks watershed moment for WNBA media rights and women's basketball visibility. First season under new media deal shows league prioritizing star power.

clusters.leadHeadline: NWSL Adds 18th Team in Columbus for $205M Expansion Fee
clusters.summary: Haslam Sports Group pays $205M for NWSL expansion slot in Columbus, doubling league size near completion. Expansion fee signals strong investor confidence and growing women's soccer market value.

clusters.leadHeadline: Kansas City Royals Strike $3B Downtown Stadium Deal with Hallmark
clusters.summary: MLB franchise secures major development partnership for new $3B stadium in unexpected downtown Kansas City location. Public-private partnership with Hallmark Cards reshapes franchise economics and city real estate.

clusters.leadHeadline: Illinois Lawmakers Push $5B Bears Stadium Plan as Deadline Looms
clusters.summary: State legislature accelerates vote on massive stadium financing package as NFL franchise decision deadline approaches. Deal could set precedent for public funding of major sports infrastructure.

clusters.leadHeadline: Chelsea Fires Coach Four Months into Six-Year Contract
clusters.summary: Premier League club terminates Liam Rosenior's deal through 2032, raising questions about contract guarantees and coaching stability. Mid-season firing highlights risks of long-term managerial commitments.

clusters.leadHeadline: NIL Dispute Over 'Associated Entities' Could Collapse College Salary Cap
clusters.summary: Definitional conflict over NIL funding sources threatens to eliminate salary cap framework in college sports. Unresolved issue could create unlimited spending environment across FBS programs.

clusters.leadHeadline: Rogers Communications Plans $18B Sports Team Portfolio Stake Sale
clusters.summary: Canadian telecom giant values sports holdings at $18B, signaling strong market appetite for team ownership stakes. Potential sale could reshape ownership landscape in North American franchises.

poll.question: Which sports league will face the biggest business challenge in 2026?
options: NFL: FCC media rights scrutiny and stadium financing debates
College sports: NIL spending spiraling out of control without salary cap
WNBA: Sustaining Caitlin Clark hype beyond rookie season
Soccer: European clubs bleeding money despite $14B valuations

predictions.statement: NFL media rights deal structure will change within 18 months due to FCC pressure on distribution model
predictions.rationale: FCC scrutiny of NFL media strategy is escalating; regulatory pressure historically forces league concessions on broadcasting access and pricing

predictions.statement: College sports NIL salary cap will collapse entirely by 2027 without legislative intervention
predictions.rationale: Definitional disputes over 'associated entities' already threatening framework; courts leaning toward athlete compensation rights expansion

predictions.statement: Chicago Bears will announce stadium deal financed through public funding by end of 2026
predictions.rationale: Illinois legislature racing against decision deadline with $5B plan; NFL ownership pressure and state political support create urgency

predictions.statement: WNBA media viewership will decline 15-25% in 2026-27 season after Caitlin Clark rookie hype fades
predictions.rationale: Unprecedented national coverage drove 2025 interest; historical precedent shows rookie stars don't sustain non-core audience growth without broader league development

predictions.statement: Rogers Communications will complete sports team stake sale by Q4 2026 at $18B valuation or higher
predictions.rationale: Market conditions favor team sales; telecom earnings signals portfolio optimization, but buyer pool for $18B stake remains limited

picks.edge: OKC is heavy favorite but Suns have nothing to lose in potential play-in/lottery scenario. OKC's 64-18 record suggests regression risk and potential bulletin board material. Suns play better when underdog mentality engaged. Thunder may overlook inferior seed. Spread too wide given NBA volatility.

Wild are home favorite with superior record (46-24-12 vs 50-20-12). Dallas has played heavy minutes late season with injury concerns. Wild have better home ice advantage in April playoff hockey. Stars' 50-win record slightly inflated; Wild's point differential stronger. Home ice + rest advantage favors Minnesota.

Oilers are slight home favorites but Ducks' 43-33-6 record masks competitive team. Edmonton's 41-30-11 is actually weaker record than appears. Ducks play well as underdog and have motivated roster. Late-season matchup suggests Ducks have more urgency. Plus-money underdog with solid goaltending matchup favors value play.

Both teams play fast-paced systems. OKC averages 113+ PPG at home. Suns will push pace despite result desperation. Late-season regular season games trend toward higher scoring as teams rest key players selectively. Thunder's elite offense vs Suns' weaker defense creates scoring environment regardless of final outcome.
